disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symmetrical, smooth, poorly-defined brown patches on sun-exposed facial areas, no texture change/scaling/itch
trigger_question_examples: Are the patches symmetrical on both sides, smooth and flat (no lumps or scaling)?
Do they blend into normal skin with blurry edges?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, they're symmetrical on both cheeks, smooth and flat with blurry edges — no lumps or scaling.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No single asymmetric/irregular/raised or textured pigment spot (atypical-lesion screen)
trigger_question_examples: Is there one particular spot that's different — asymmetric, raised, changing, or a mix of odd colours?
Any single mole standing out from the rest?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it's all symmetrical flat brown patches — there's no single odd, raised or changing spot.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No systemic features (weakness, dizziness, vomiting, low BP, or darkening of palm creases/gums) — systemic-pigmentation screen
trigger_question_examples: Any weakness, dizziness, vomiting, or darkening of your palm creases, gums or scars?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I feel well — no weakness, dizziness or vomiting, and my palms and gums look normal.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Please just tell me it's not spreading skin cancer, I'm having a panic attack.
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: De-escalate calmly; after confirming the symmetrical benign pattern and screening for a single atypical lesion and systemic features, reassure clearly that this is benign melasma, and give sun-protection advice and OCP review. Support the acute anxiety; keep a skin-check safety-net for any single changing lesion.
